name: Chris Davison
title: Executive Leader, Business Transformation & Strategic Development
aboutBio: Proven, strategic-minded, solution-based executive with a history in leadership positions of increasing responsibility. Extensive experience in creating, developing and leading high-performing organisations in multiple business units and functions across various maturity levels. This includes leading global and culturally diverse teams while transforming the business through organic growth and through merger, acquisition, or new strategic partnership deals. Forward-thinking executive with diverse experience managing all aspects of Operations and offering unique global perspectives with demonstrated experience developing strategies for growth through innovation, market share, and cost competitiveness. Recognised multiple times with Verizon's highest performance and leadership awards including the coveted Verizon Credo Award. Focused extensively on leadership impact, employee development, and employee satisfaction.

recommendations.text: Chris is a proven executive who combines strategic clarity with genuine focus on people development. He has consistently delivered results across very different business contexts — consumer, enterprise, and government — which is rare. His ability to lead through transformation while maintaining team culture is exceptional.

socialPosts.text: Readiness was something I learned and practiced over 28 years at Verizon. It was ingrained in our culture. It showed up in a big way when the pandemic struck in 2020 and Verizon was ranked #1 by Forbes Magazine as a top employer responding to the global disaster. With disruption being the norm — whether tech, economic, or social — being ready is not an option. It is an antidote to the decision paralysis that slows down many large corporations.

How often do we have clear exit policies in place from the moment of hiring, ensuring that expectations are set from day one? It is not a question of if — it is a question of when. A well-managed exit is crucial not just for the organisation but also for the individual. Leaving with professionalism and grace ensures smooth transitions and preserves strong relationships.

I am completing my first week at a new opportunity and I want to express how much the Verizon chapter meant. 28 years. Multiple transformations. Thousands of colleagues. The relationships and lessons from that journey are something I carry forward. Grateful for every challenge and every person who made it possible.

visionQuotes: Readiness is not a response to disruption — it is an antidote to the decision paralysis that slows down large corporations.
Innovation gives us the opportunity to make the lives of those around us better.
Leadership impact, employee development, and employee satisfaction are not soft metrics — they are the foundation of sustainable performance.
strategies: Build organisational readiness as a cultural discipline, not a crisis response
Lead transformation through people — develop talent and create conditions for high-performing teams to operate autonomously
Drive cost competitiveness and market share growth simultaneously through operational excellence
Manage vendor relationships strategically — not as procurement transactions but as value partnerships
Maintain performance excellence through clear values and consistent leadership behaviour across all business maturity levels
challenges: Navigating large-organisation complexity across consumer, business, and government operations with very different customer dynamics
Leading teams through merger and acquisition integration while maintaining culture and performance
Balancing cost competitiveness imperatives with investment in people development and innovation
Managing vendor ecosystems of increasing complexity across a diversifying technology portfolio
Sustaining readiness and agility in a large corporation that can drift toward process inertia
opportunities: Apply 28 years of Verizon transformation experience in a new context — advisory, board, or senior executive role
Lead the next phase of business transformation at a company navigating technology disruption
Build on government operations experience to address the growing market for public sector digital transformation
Leverage deep vendor management expertise to help organisations rationalise and optimise complex supplier ecosystems
Develop leadership and culture programmes based on Verizon Credo Award-recognised leadership approach
trendsInsights: Business transformation at scale requires both technological and cultural readiness — most organisations underinvest in the latter
Vendor management is becoming a strategic capability as technology ecosystems grow more complex and interdependent
Government operations technology is undergoing rapid modernisation driven by public expectations set by consumer digital experiences
Organisational agility is the key differentiator between companies that navigate disruption well and those that do not
Leadership development is increasingly recognised as a direct driver of business performance, not just a HR function
competitorsPartners: AT&T Business, T-Mobile for Business (Verizon competitors)
Major technology vendors across consumer, business and government operations
Strategic advisory and transformation consulting ecosystem
productsTechnologies: Verizon enterprise and consumer operations platforms
Vendor management and strategic sourcing systems
Business transformation and Lean Six Sigma methodologies
Consumer and business digital service platforms
targetMarkets: Large enterprise organisations undergoing business transformation
Organisations navigating merger and acquisition integration
Companies seeking operational excellence and cost competitiveness
kpiMetrics: 28 years of progressive leadership at Verizon across Consumer, Business, and Government segments
Verizon Credo Award recipient — multiple occasions (Verizon's highest performance and leadership recognition)
Verizon ranked #1 by Forbes as top employer responding to COVID-19 pandemic (2020)
Led teams through multiple business transformations, mergers, and strategic partnership integrations
Consistent track record of high employee satisfaction and team performance metrics
decisionInsights: Readiness-oriented — makes decisions with long-term organisational resilience as a primary criterion
People-first — consistently prioritises employee development and culture in strategic decisions
Experience-grounded — 28 years of large-organisation leadership provides a deep pattern library for complex decisions
Values-driven — Verizon Credo Award recognition signals decisions aligned with organisational values, not just outcomes
Transformation-experienced — comfortable making large-scale decisions in ambiguous, high-change environments
risksThreats: Transition risk following a long tenure at a single organisation — new contexts require fresh frameworks
Technology disruption moving faster than traditional large-organisation transformation cycles
Vendor ecosystem complexity increasing the cost and time required for strategic sourcing decisions
Talent development investment under pressure from short-term cost reduction mandates
Organisational inertia in large corporations resisting transformation despite strategic necessity
quotes: Readiness was something I learned and practiced over 28 years at Verizon. With disruption being the norm, being ready is not an option.
It is not a question of if — it is a question of when. A well-managed exit ensures smooth transitions and preserves strong relationships.
bio: Chris Davison is an Executive Leader at Verizon with focus on Business Transformation, Strategic Development, Vendor Management, and Consumer, Business, and Government Operations. With 28 years at Verizon, he built a track record of leading high-performing organisations through organic growth, mergers, acquisitions, and strategic partnership transformations. He is a multiple Verizon Credo Award recipient — the company's highest performance and leadership recognition — and has been recognised for his focus on leadership impact, employee development, and organisational culture.
